title: Troubleshooting Interpersonal Effectiveness Skills

Interpersonal Effectiveness Worksheet 7   (I. E. Handout 9) (p. 1 of 2)
Troubleshooting Interpersonal Effectiveness Skills
Due Date:             Name:                                    Week Starting:
Fill out this sheet whenever you practice your interpersonal skills and whenever you have an oppor-
tunity to practice, even if you don‚Äôt (or almost don‚Äôt) do anything to practice. Write on the back of this
sheet if you need more room.
Do I have the skills I need? Check out the instructions.
1
	Review what has already been tried.
‚ÄĘ‚ÄĘ Do I know how to be skillful in getting what I want?
‚ÄĘ‚ÄĘ Do I know how to say what I want to say?
‚ÄĘ‚ÄĘ Did I follow the skill instructions to the letter?

¬Č Not sure:

¬Č Wrote out what I wanted to say first.

¬Č Reread the instructions.

¬Č Got coaching from someone I trust.

¬Č Practiced with a friend or in front of a mirror.
Did it work the next time?  ÔĀĪ Yes (Fabulous)  ÔĀĪ No (Continue)  ÔĀĪ Didn‚Äôt try again

¬Č Yes:
Do I know what I really want in this interaction?
2
	Ask:
‚ÄĘ‚ÄĘ Am I undecided about what I really want in this interaction?
‚ÄĘ‚ÄĘ Am I ambivalent about my priorities?
‚ÄĘ‚ÄĘ Am I having trouble balancing:
‚ÄĘ‚ÄĘ Asking for too much versus not asking for anything?
‚ÄĘ‚ÄĘ Saying no to everything versus giving in to everything?
‚ÄĘ‚ÄĘ Is fear or shame getting in the way of knowing what I really want?

¬Č Not sure:

¬Č Did pros and cons to compare different objectives.

¬Č Used emotion regulation skills to reduce fear and shame.
Did this help?  ÔĀĪ Yes (Fabulous)  ÔĀĪ No (Continue)  ÔĀĪ Didn‚Äôt try again

¬Č Yes:
Are my short-term goals getting in the way of my long-term goals?
3
	Ask:
‚ÄĘ‚ÄĘ Is ‚Äúnow, now, now‚ÄĚ winning out over getting what I really want?
‚ÄĘ‚ÄĘ Is emotion mind controlling what I say and do instead of Wise Mind?

¬Č Yes:

¬Č Did a pros and cons comparing short-term to long-term goals.

¬Č Waited until another time when I‚Äôm not in emotion mind.
Did this help?  ÔĀĪ Yes (Fabulous)  ÔĀĪ No (Continue)  ÔĀĪ Didn‚Äôt try again

¬Č No:
(continued on next page)

179
Interpersonal Effectiveness Worksheet 7   (p. 2 of 2)
Are my emotions getting in the way of using my skills?
4
	Ask:
‚ÄĘ‚ÄĘ Do I get too upset to use my skills?
‚ÄĘ‚ÄĘ Are my emotions so high that I am over my skills breakdown point?

¬Č Yes:

¬Č Tried TIP skills.

¬Č Used self-soothing crisis survival skills before the interaction to get myself calm.

¬Č Did mindfulness of current emotions (Emotion Regulation Handout 22).

¬Č Refocused attention completely on the present objective.
Did this help?  ÔĀĪ Yes (Fabulous)  ÔĀĪ No (Continue)  ÔĀĪ Didn‚Äôt try again

¬Č No:
Are worries, assumptions, and myths getting in my way?
5
	Ask:
‚ÄĘ‚ÄĘ Are thoughts about bad consequences blocking my action?
‚ÄúThey won‚Äôt like me,‚ÄĚ ‚ÄúShe will think I am stupid.‚ÄĚ
‚ÄĘ‚ÄĘ Are thoughts about whether I deserve to get what I want in my way?
‚ÄúI am such a bad person I don‚Äôt deserve this.‚ÄĚ
‚ÄĘ‚ÄĘ Am I calling myself names that stop me from doing anything?
‚ÄúI won‚Äôt do it right,‚ÄĚ ‚ÄúI‚Äôll probably fall apart,‚ÄĚ ‚ÄúI‚Äôm so stupid.‚ÄĚ
‚ÄĘ‚ÄĘ Am I believing myths about interpersonal effectiveness?
‚ÄúIf I make a request, this will show that I am a very weak person,‚ÄĚ ‚ÄúOnly wimps have values.‚ÄĚ

¬Č Yes:

¬Č Challenged myths.

¬Č Checked the facts.

¬Č Did opposite action all the way.
Did this help?  ÔĀĪ Yes (Fabulous)  ÔĀĪ No (Continue)  ÔĀĪ Didn‚Äôt try again

¬Č No:
Is the environment more powerful than my skills?
6
	Ask:
‚ÄĘ‚ÄĘ Are the people who have what I want or need more powerful than I am?
‚ÄĘ‚ÄĘ Are the people commanding me powerful and in control?
‚ÄĘ‚ÄĘ Will others be threatened if I get what I want?
‚ÄĘ‚ÄĘ Do others have reasons for not liking me if I get what I want?

¬Č Yes:

¬Č Tried problem solving.

¬Č Found a powerful ally.

¬Č Practiced radical acceptance.
Did this help?  ÔĀĪ Yes (Fabulous)  ÔĀĪ No (Continue)  ÔĀĪ Didn‚Äôt try again

¬Č No:

ourspace_shared_life_filter.title: Troubleshooting Interpersonal Effectiveness Skills
ourspace_shared_life_filter.shared_facing_summary: Use this to protect both people: needs are real, boundaries are kind, and a pause is not abandonment when repair is named.
tiny_steps: Name the ask in one sentence.
Agree on a return time or next check-in.

flare_day_versions.minimum: One breath, one label, one safe message, or one checkbox.
flare_day_versions.medium: Two to five minutes with everything reachable from bed/chair.
flare_day_versions.full: Use the original worksheet/handout when both bodies and brains have capacity.

accessibility_modifications: Use text/chat if speech is hard.
Include caregiver capacity as part of the plan, not an afterthought.
relationship_and_caregiver_notes: William's needs and Jasper's capacity both count.
Ask before helping; ask before escalating; name the smallest next step.
Pause means pause, not abandonment. I am coming back. I care about us. I need a smaller next step.
What is needed, what capacity exists, what can be smaller, and what can wait?
identity_and_trauma_filter: Use William's masculine language and Jasper's they/them language.
Use consent-based, non-shaming, body-neutral language unless a person has explicitly chosen a term.
Do not turn symptoms, dysphoria, trauma responses, clutter, fatigue, or emotional intensity into moral failure.
avoid_or_adjust: Do not use skills to win, corner, threaten leaving, or make either person prove pain or disability.
